question: Engineers receive…………………to develop skills for aerospace projects.
Flexibility and………..help employees adjust to fast-changing environments.
Technology allows us to.…….. information instantly at the click of a button.
Our schools ……………..old textbooks with new editions every year.
A good student must………..to new situations to succeed academically.
Many products in the market are ………………..rather than natural.
Teachers encourage ……………..in students to solve problems creatively.
People must stay ………when interacting with strangers or new situations.
A………………can complete repetitive tasks automatically in factories.
…………… means the ability to produce new and original ideas or works.
After getting married, she needs to…………………to life in the new city.
The word “visitors” refers to ………………….. .
The visitors wanted the merchants to .............................. .
options: be with them in their problem
judge which one of the two is more beautiful
correct_answer: judge which one of the two is more beautiful

question: According to the passage, the way to solve a problem is to……………………. .
The underlined word “appreciated” means .............................. .
The moral of the text is ................................... .
options: we should do our best to overcome poverty
wealth is more beautiful than poverty
We can solve difficult situations by calm thinking
correct_answer: We can solve difficult situations by calm thinking

question: The merchant feared to declare that wealth is more beautiful than poverty because ..... .
The underlined word “declare” is the antonym of .............................. .
Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: Sama started learning English in 2009. (for)
correct_answer: Sama has been learning English for 16 years.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: Mariam went to Italy and came back. (been to)
Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: Adham has lived in Alexandria for five years. (since)
correct_answer: Adham has lived in Alexandria since 2020.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: Nada last saw her cousin two weeks ago. (hasn't)
correct_answer: Nada hasn't seen her cousin for two weeks.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: He finished his lunch a moment ago. (just)
correct_answer: He has just finished his lunch.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: Mo Salah has played for Liverpool since 2017. (for)
correct_answer: Mo Salah has played for Liverpool for 8 years.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: He practiced swimming daily when he was young. (since)
correct_answer: He has practiced swimming daily since he was young.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: Ali went to Aswan. He came back home. (has been)
Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: I last saw Ahmed two years ago. (haven't)
correct_answer: I haven't seen Ahmed for two years.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: I met my uncle a few minutes ago. (just)
correct_answer: I have just met my uncle.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: It's been a long time since I last saw Heba. (haven't)
correct_answer: I haven't seen Heba for a long time.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: They watched the match a short time ago. (just)
correct_answer: They have just watched the match.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: This is the most beautiful girl I have ever seen. (never)
correct_answer: I have never seen such a beautiful girl.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: Ibrahim went to the market and she is still there. (gone)
correct_answer: Ibrahim has gone to the market.

question: Rewrite the following sentence using the word in brackets: The last time I saw Hassan was in June. (since)
correct_answer: I haven't seen Hassan since June.
